_notes.why_enableWeakerNestedSandbox: Anthropic's docs note this flag is for running inside Docker without privileged namespaces. Apple Container's microVM gives us kernel isolation externally, so accepting the 'weakened' inner sandbox (whatever that means exactly) is a reasonable tradeoff — we still have the outer kernel boundary doing the real work.
_notes.why_failIfUnavailable_false: If bubblewrap fails inside the container, we want Claude Code to still run (it has the outer kernel boundary as defense). For a stricter posture flip this to true — it'll hard-fail if the inner sandbox can't start.
_notes.why_allowUnsandboxedCommands_false: Matches Tier 0 unattended recommendation: structurally disable the escape hatch that the March 2026 Falco demo showed Claude will request on its own.
_notes.network_enforcement: Anthropic's proxy enforces this allowlist at the bash subprocess level. No iptables, no Squid, no host-side pf rules needed. Proxy does NOT inspect HTTPS payloads — domain-fronting and exfiltration via broadly-allowed domains (github.com via gist) remain possible.
